pages.full_text:
We drove to Certosa + the fridge is working again. Certosa is a monastery set up in [?C4] by Visconti family. It still has 6 monks. Beautiful carvings, inlays, frescoes.

[right margin annotations alongside aerial photo:]
25 little houses for ~~most~~ monks.
food put through hatch in wall
downstairs Sitting room chapel garden
up stairs bedroom loggia
pages.notes: The text reads 'set up in [?C4] by Visconti family' — the notation after 'in' is unclear; likely a century abbreviation, possibly 'C14' (14th century) as Certosa di Pavia was founded 1396. Strikethrough on 'most' in '25 little houses for ~~most~~ monks' — the word 'most' is crossed out. No handwritten page number visible on this page.

pages.full_text:
Cisterian order founded by St. Bruno (10
work, prayer, meditation, silence, study

[below top photo, left side:]
Small cloister
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 71 in top right corner. The text 'Cisterian order founded by St. Bruno (10' is likely a note about the founding — the diary writer writes 'Cisterian' but Certosa di Pavia is Carthusian (founded by St. Bruno in 1084); the diarist may be confusing Cistercian/Carthusian, or the '(10' may refer to the founding date (10[84]). The text is cut off after '(10'. Left margin of this page shows partial text fragments ('ttle', 'for', 'r', 'in', 'is', 'room', 'r', 'l') which are the left-side annotations from the facing page visible through gutter overlap — not transcribed twice. Two glued-in postcard/photos: top shows a monk sculpture in an ornate inlaid wooden doorway; bottom shows exterior of the Certosa monastery building.
